items.description:
A single deployment definition as written in `config.yaml`.
One deployment maps to one cloud region/provider combination and owns
a set of named services.

deployment_name.description: Unique identifier for this deployment. Used in CLI `--target` flags and state files.
deployment_name.title: deployment_name

duplicate_deployment_name.description:
When set, this deployment is treated as a copy of the named deployment.
All services and settings are inherited and can be overridden.
duplicate_deployment_name.title: duplicate_deployment_name

env.description: Environment variables injected into every service in this deployment.
env.title: env

env_file.description: Path to a `.env` file whose contents are injected into every service.
env_file.title: env_file

location.description: Provider-specific datacenter/region slug, e.g. `"ash"` for Hetzner Ashburn.
location.title: location

pre_deployment.description: Commands run once on the relay server before any service setup begins.

file.description: Path to a shell script file on the target server to execute instead of `cmds`.
file.title: file

work_dir.description: Working directory for the commands or script.
work_dir.title: work_dir

pre_deployment.title: pre_deployment

relay_server_options.description: Options for the relay server provisioned for each deployment.

server_type.description: Override the default relay server hardware type (e.g. `"cx22"` on Hetzner).
server_type.title: server_type

relay_server_options.title: relay_server_options

additionalProperties.description:
Configuration for a single named service within a deployment.
A service represents one logical tier (e.g. `web`, `api`, `lb`).
The `type` field selects the deployment path; all other fields are
interpreted in the context of that type.

allow_ips.description:
Restrict inbound requests to these IPs/CIDRs only. All other IPs are
denied. Applies to all server blocks on this load balancer.

Accepts raw IPs/CIDRs or named providers which are automatically
expanded to their published ranges. Supported providers: "cloudflare".

Example: ["cloudflare", "5.78.1.245"]

Only applies to `load_balancer` services.
allow_ips.title: allow_ips

clusters.description:
Number of server clusters. During rolling updates, clusters are
deployed one at a time for zero-downtime. Defaults to `1`.
clusters.title: clusters

dependencies.description:
Packages to install on provisioned servers before running flight scripts.
Use `apt` for system packages, `turboci` for managed runtimes (bun, node, docker).
dependencies.title: dependencies

dir_mappings.description: Local → remote directory mappings synced before each deployment.

items.description: A local → remote directory sync mapping used during the prepare phase.

dst.description: Absolute destination path on target servers.
dst.title: dst

ignore_file.description: Path to an rsync-style ignore file.
ignore_file.title: ignore_file

ignore_patterns.description: Explicit patterns to exclude from the sync.
ignore_patterns.title: ignore_patterns

relay_ignore.description: Patterns excluded only on the local → relay leg of the sync.
relay_ignore.title: relay_ignore

src.description: Local source path (relative to the `.turboci` directory).
src.title: src

use_gitignore.description: If `true`, applies the project's `.gitignore` as rsync exclude rules.
use_gitignore.title: use_gitignore

dir_mappings.title: dir_mappings

duplicate_service_name.description:
When set, this service is treated as a copy of the named service
within the same deployment.
duplicate_service_name.title: duplicate_service_name

enable_public_ip.description:
When `true`, servers for this service receive a public IP in addition to
their private IP. Defaults to `false` — servers are private-only and
accessed exclusively through the relay.
enable_public_ip.title: enable_public_ip

env.description: Environment variables injected into this service's servers.
env.title: env

env_file.description: Path to a `.env` file loaded into this service's environment.
env_file.title: env_file

anyOf.description: Configuration for pulling a git repository onto target servers.

api_key.description: API key or personal access token for private repo access.
api_key.title: api_key

host.description: Custom git host, used for self-hosted Gitea/Gitlab instances.
host.title: host

keep_updated.description:
If `true`, TurboCI continuously polls the repository and re-runs
the flight scripts whenever new commits are detected.
keep_updated.title: keep_updated

paradigm.description: Git hosting provider. Affects clone URL construction and auth.
paradigm.title: paradigm

public_repo.description: If `true`, clones without authentication (no deploy key required).
public_repo.title: public_repo

work_dir.description:
Absolute path on target servers where the repo is cloned.
Defaults to `/app`.
work_dir.title: work_dir

items.description: Configuration for pulling a git repository onto target servers.

api_key.description: API key or personal access token for private repo access.
api_key.title: api_key

host.description: Custom git host, used for self-hosted Gitea/Gitlab instances.
host.title: host

keep_updated.description:
If `true`, TurboCI continuously polls the repository and re-runs
the flight scripts whenever new commits are detected.
keep_updated.title: keep_updated

paradigm.description: Git hosting provider. Affects clone URL construction and auth.
paradigm.title: paradigm

public_repo.description: If `true`, clones without authentication (no deploy key required).
public_repo.title: public_repo

work_dir.description:
Absolute path on target servers where the repo is cloned.
Defaults to `/app`.
work_dir.title: work_dir

git.description: Git repository source(s) to clone/pull onto target servers.
git.title: git

haproxy.description:
HAProxy proxy configuration (frontends, backends, health checks, stats).
Only used when `type` is `"haproxy"`.

balance.description:
Load balancing algorithm.
- `roundrobin` — each server in turn (default)
- `leastconn` — server with fewest active connections (best for databases)
- `first` — first available server (useful for active/passive failover)
- `source` — sticky sessions based on client IP
balance.title: balance

listen_port.description:
Port HAProxy's frontend listens on for client connections.
Defaults to `5432` for postgres mode, `3306` for mysql mode.
listen_port.title: listen_port

mode.description:
Proxy mode.
- `tcp` — layer-4 pass-through, required for databases (default)
- `http` — layer-7 HTTP proxy with header inspection
mode.title: mode

stats_password.description: Password for the HAProxy statistics page.
stats_password.title: stats_password

stats_port.description: Port for the HAProxy statistics web page. Defaults to `8404`.
stats_port.title: stats_port

stats_user.description: Username for the HAProxy statistics page.
stats_user.title: stats_user

target_services.description:
Backend services HAProxy will proxy traffic to.
Accepts any service type (postgres, mysql, mariadb-galera, etc.).

items.description:
A single backend server entry for an HAProxy service.
HAProxy is a generic TCP/HTTP proxy and can route to any service type.

backup.description: If `true`, this server is only used when all non-backup servers are down.
backup.title: backup

check.description: Health check settings for this backend server.

fall.description: Number of consecutive failures required to mark a server DOWN. Defaults to `3`.
fall.title: fall

interval.description:
Time between health checks (e.g. `"2s"`, `"500ms"`).
Defaults to `"2s"`.
interval.title: interval

rise.description: Number of consecutive successes required to mark a server UP. Defaults to `2`.
rise.title: rise

type.description:
Health check protocol.
- `tcp` — basic TCP connection check (works for any service)
- `http` — HTTP request check
- `mysql` — MySQL/MariaDB protocol handshake check
- `pgsql` — PostgreSQL startup message check
- `redis` — Redis PING check
type.title: type

check.title: check

port.description: Port the target service listens on.
port.title: port

service_name.description: Name of the service in the same deployment to proxy traffic to.
service_name.title: service_name

target_services.title: target_services

haproxy.title: haproxy

healthcheck.description: Healthcheck run after the service starts to verify it is live.
healthcheck.title: healthcheck

init.description: Shell commands run once on first deploy (not on re-deploys).
init.title: init

instances.description: Number of servers to provision per cluster. Defaults to `1`.
instances.title: instances

logs.description:
Commands or log sources tailed when `turboci control` streams logs
for this service.
logs.title: logs

mariadb_galera.description:
MariaDB Galera cluster configuration (cluster name, SST method, databases).
Only used when `type` is `"mariadb-galera"`.

bind_address.description:
Address to bind the MySQL listener to.
Use `"0.0.0.0"` to accept connections from all interfaces (required for
MaxScale or HAProxy to reach private cluster IPs). Defaults to `"0.0.0.0"`.
bind_address.title: bind_address

cluster_name.description:
`wsrep_cluster_name` — shared identifier all Galera nodes must agree on.
Defaults to `"turboci_galera_cluster"`.
cluster_name.title: cluster_name

databases.description: Databases and users to create after cluster bootstrap.

items.description:
A database and optional user to create during service initialisation.
Used by `mariadb-galera`, `postgres`, and `mysql` service types.

charset.description: Character set (e.g. `"utf8mb4"`). Only applies to MySQL/MariaDB.
charset.title: charset

user.description: Database user to create and grant privileges to this database.
user.title: user

databases.title: databases

port.description: MySQL-protocol port the cluster nodes listen on. Defaults to `3306`.
port.title: port

sst_method.description:
State Snapshot Transfer method used when a new node joins the cluster.
- `mariabackup` — hot backup via MariaDB Backup (recommended, no locks)
- `rsync` — rsync-based full copy (simple, blocks writes during transfer)
- `xtrabackup-v2` — Percona XtraBackup (for MySQL-based setups)
sst_method.title: sst_method

mariadb_galera.title: mariadb_galera

maxscale.description:
MaxScale proxy configuration (routers, monitors, admin API).
Only used when `type` is `"maxscale"`.

admin_port.description: Port for the MaxScale REST API and GUI. Defaults to `8989`.
admin_port.title: admin_port

admin_user.description: MaxScale admin REST API username. Defaults to `"admin"`.
admin_user.title: admin_user

listen_port.description:
MySQL-protocol port MaxScale listens on for client connections.
Defaults to `3306`.
listen_port.title: listen_port

monitor.description:
MaxScale monitor module.
- `galeramon` — for MariaDB Galera clusters (detects primary/donor/joiner state)
- `mariadbmon` — for MariaDB/MySQL primary-replica setups
- `mysqlmon` — legacy monitor for older MySQL setups
monitor.title: monitor

password.description: Password for the MaxScale database user.
password.title: password

router.description:
MaxScale router module.
- `readwritesplit` — routes writes to primary, distributes reads across replicas (default)
- `readconnroute` — simple round-robin connection routing
- `schemarouter` — routes by schema name (sharding)
router.title: router

target_services.description:
Backend services MaxScale will route connections to.
Typically points to a `mariadb-galera` or `mysql` service.

items.description:
A single backend server entry for a MaxScale proxy service.
Points to a `mariadb-galera` or `mysql` service in the same deployment.

backup.description: If `true`, this target is used only when all non-backup servers are unavailable.
backup.title: backup

port.description: MySQL-protocol port the target service listens on (typically `3306`).
port.title: port

service_name.description: Name of the service in the same deployment to route DB traffic to.
service_name.title: service_name

weight.description: Relative weight for connection distribution. Higher values receive more connections.
weight.title: weight

target_services.title: target_services

user.description:
Database user MaxScale uses for monitoring and service authentication.
This user must have `REPLICATION CLIENT`, `SHOW DATABASES`, and `SELECT` privileges.
user.title: user

maxscale.title: maxscale

mysql.description:
MySQL server configuration (port, users, replication).
Only used when `type` is `"mysql"`.

bind_address.description:
Address MySQL binds to.
Use `"0.0.0.0"` to accept connections from all interfaces (required for
ProxySQL or HAProxy to reach private server IPs). Defaults to `"0.0.0.0"`.
bind_address.title: bind_address

databases.description: Databases and users to create after initialisation.

items.description:
A database and optional user to create during service initialisation.
Used by `mariadb-galera`, `postgres`, and `mysql` service types.

charset.description: Character set (e.g. `"utf8mb4"`). Only applies to MySQL/MariaDB.
charset.title: charset

user.description: Database user to create and grant privileges to this database.
user.title: user

databases.title: databases

max_connections.description: Maximum number of simultaneous client connections. Defaults to `151`.
max_connections.title: max_connections

enabled.description: Enable MySQL binary-log replication. Defaults to `false`.
enabled.title: enabled

server_id.title: server_id

user.description: Replication user created on the primary. Defaults to `"replicator"`.
user.title: user

mysql.title: mysql

os.description: OS image slug to use when creating servers for this service.
os.title: os

postgres.description:
PostgreSQL server configuration (port, users, replication).
Only used when `type` is `"postgres"`.

databases.description: Databases and users to create after initialisation.

items.description:
A database and optional user to create during service initialisation.
Used by `mariadb-galera`, `postgres`, and `mysql` service types.

charset.description: Character set (e.g. `"utf8mb4"`). Only applies to MySQL/MariaDB.
charset.title: charset

user.description: Database user to create and grant privileges to this database.
user.title: user

databases.title: databases

listen_addresses.description:
Interfaces PostgreSQL listens on.
Use `"*"` to accept connections from all interfaces (required for HAProxy
to reach private server IPs). Defaults to `"localhost"`.
listen_addresses.title: listen_addresses

max_connections.description:
Maximum number of client connections. Defaults to `100`.
Increase when using a connection pooler like PgBouncer or HAProxy.
max_connections.title: max_connections

port.description: Port PostgreSQL listens on. Defaults to `5432`.
port.title: port

max_wal_senders.description:
Maximum number of concurrent WAL sender processes.
Should be ≥ the number of replica instances. Defaults to `5`.
max_wal_senders.title: max_wal_senders

user.description: Replication user created on the primary. Defaults to `"replicator"`.
user.title: user

shared_buffers.description:
Shared memory buffer size (e.g. `"256MB"`, `"1GB"`).
Rule of thumb: set to 25% of available RAM. Defaults to `"128MB"`.
shared_buffers.title: shared_buffers

postgres.title: postgres

proxysql.description:
ProxySQL proxy configuration (hostgroups, query rules, monitor user).
Only used when `type` is `"proxysql"`.

admin_port.description:
ProxySQL admin interface port (MySQL protocol, admin credentials required).
Defaults to `6032`.
admin_port.title: admin_port

listen_port.description:
MySQL-protocol port clients connect to on ProxySQL.
Defaults to `6033`.
listen_port.title: listen_port

monitor_password.description: Password for the ProxySQL monitor user.
monitor_password.title: monitor_password

monitor_user.description:
MySQL user ProxySQL uses to monitor backend health via `SELECT 1` queries.
This user needs `USAGE` privilege on the backend servers.
monitor_user.title: monitor_user

query_rules.description:
Query routing rules evaluated against incoming query digests.
Rules are applied in ascending `rule_id` order.

items.description:
A query routing rule for ProxySQL.
Rules are evaluated in ascending `rule_id` order; the first matching rule wins.

apply.description:
If `true`, stop evaluating further rules once this rule matches.
Defaults to `true`.
apply.title: apply

comment.description: Human-readable annotation stored in the ProxySQL rule table.
comment.title: comment

destination_hostgroup.description: Hostgroup to send matching queries to.
destination_hostgroup.title: destination_hostgroup

match_digest.description:
Regular expression matched against the normalised query digest.
Example: `"^SELECT"` matches all SELECT statements.
match_digest.title: match_digest

rule_id.description:
Numeric rule identifier. Lower numbers are evaluated first.
Defaults to insertion order if omitted.
rule_id.title: rule_id

query_rules.title: query_rules

reader_hostgroup.description:
Hostgroup ID for read traffic (SELECT).
Defaults to `20`.
reader_hostgroup.title: reader_hostgroup

target_services.description:
Backend services ProxySQL will route connections to.
Typically points to a `mysql` service. Use different `hostgroup` values
on target entries to separate writer and reader pools.

items.description:
A single backend server entry for a ProxySQL service.
Maps to one `mysql_servers` row in the ProxySQL admin database.

backup.description: If `true`, this server is placed in the OFFLINE_SOFT state initially.
backup.title: backup

hostgroup.description:
ProxySQL hostgroup ID. Use separate hostgroups for writers and readers
(e.g. writers: `10`, readers: `20`) to enable read/write splitting via query rules.
Defaults to `0`.
hostgroup.title: hostgroup

max_connections.description: Maximum number of connections ProxySQL will open to this backend server.
max_connections.title: max_connections

port.description: MySQL-protocol port the target service listens on (typically `3306`).
port.title: port

service_name.description: Name of the service in the same deployment to route traffic to.
service_name.title: service_name

weight.description: Relative connection weight within the hostgroup.
weight.title: weight

target_services.title: target_services

writer_hostgroup.description:
Hostgroup ID for write traffic (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DDL).
Defaults to `10`.
writer_hostgroup.title: writer_hostgroup

proxysql.title: proxysql

run.description: Preflight, start, and postflight shell scripts for this service.

postflight.description: Commands run after the service starts (smoke tests, cache warm-up, etc.).

file.description: Path to a shell script file on the target server to execute instead of `cmds`.
file.title: file

work_dir.description: Working directory for the commands or script.
work_dir.title: work_dir

postflight.title: postflight

preflight.description: Commands run before the service starts (install deps, migrations, etc.).

file.description: Path to a shell script file on the target server to execute instead of `cmds`.
file.title: file

work_dir.description: Working directory for the commands or script.
work_dir.title: work_dir

preflight.title: preflight

start.description: Commands that start the service process (must be non-blocking or daemonised).

file.description: Path to a shell script file on the target server to execute instead of `cmds`.
file.title: file

work_dir.description: Working directory for the commands or script.
work_dir.title: work_dir

start.title: start

work_dir.description: Default working directory for all scripts in this run block.
work_dir.title: work_dir

run.title: run

spec_url.description:
Yaml file url which defines the spec, used only when `type` is
set to `spec`. This can also be a path to a local file; absolute
and relative paths work the same.
spec_url.title: spec_url

ssl.description: SSL/TLS configuration. Triggers Certbot certificate provisioning.
ssl.title: ssl

target_services.description:
Target services this load balancer proxies to.
Only used when `type` is `"load_balancer"`.

items.description:
A single upstream target for a load balancer service.
Each target maps to one NGINX `upstream` block and one `server` block.

backup.description: If `true`, this target is used as a fallback when all primary servers are down.
backup.title: backup

domains.description:
Domains to attach to this target's server block. Triggers TLS termination
when `ssl` is configured on the parent service. Accepts plain strings or
`TCIConfigServiceDomain` objects.

anyOf.description: An explicitly named domain, used as an alternative to a plain domain string.

domains.title: domains

locations.description:
Custom NGINX `location` blocks rendered inside this target's server block.
The default `location /` proxy block is auto-generated unless a `"/"` path
is explicitly defined here.

items.description:
A custom NGINX `location` block rendered inside a load balancer server block.
By default each location proxies to the upstream; set `proxy: false` for
locations that should be handled locally (static files, redirects, etc.).

directives.description:
Arbitrary NGINX directives added verbatim inside this location block.
String values emit a single directive; array values emit one line per entry.

Example:
```yaml
directives:
  root: /var/www/html
  add_header:
    - "Cache-Control 'public'"
    - "X-Frame-Options DENY"
```
directives.title: directives

match.description:
NGINX location path or pattern.
Supports all NGINX matching prefixes: `"/"`, `"/api/"`, `"= /ping"`,
`"~* \\.(jpg|png)$"`, etc.
match.title: match

no_proxy.description:
If `true`, the upstream `proxy_pass` block is omitted for this location.
Use when serving static files, issuing redirects, or returning custom
responses. Defaults to `false`.
no_proxy.title: no_proxy

rate_limit.description: Rate limiting applied to this location.

burst.description: Burst queue size — requests exceeding the rate are queued up to this limit.
burst.title: burst

key.description:
NGINX rate limit key. Defaults to `"$binary_remote_addr"` (per client IP).
Can be any NGINX variable, e.g. `"$http_x_forwarded_for"`.
key.title: key

nodelay.description: If `true`, burst requests are served immediately rather than delayed.
nodelay.title: nodelay

rate_limit.title: rate_limit

target_path.description:
Path to target on the upstream. If left empty, the default path
will be used.
target_path.title: target_path

target_port.description:
Port on the target upstream to target. Default port is the one
set on the parent `target_services` onject.
target_port.title: target_port

locations.title: locations

port.description: Port the target service listens on.
port.title: port

service_name.description: Name of the service in the same deployment to proxy traffic to.
service_name.title: service_name

target_location.description: Custom NGINX `location` block added to the base("/") location

directives.description:
Arbitrary NGINX directives added verbatim inside this location block.
String values emit a single directive; array values emit one line per entry.

Example:
```yaml
directives:
  root: /var/www/html
  add_header:
    - "Cache-Control 'public'"
    - "X-Frame-Options DENY"
```
directives.title: directives

match.description:
NGINX location path or pattern.
Supports all NGINX matching prefixes: `"/"`, `"/api/"`, `"= /ping"`,
`"~* \\.(jpg|png)$"`, etc.
match.title: match

no_proxy.description:
If `true`, the upstream `proxy_pass` block is omitted for this location.
Use when serving static files, issuing redirects, or returning custom
responses. Defaults to `false`.
no_proxy.title: no_proxy

rate_limit.description: Rate limiting applied to this location.

burst.description: Burst queue size — requests exceeding the rate are queued up to this limit.
burst.title: burst

key.description:
NGINX rate limit key. Defaults to `"$binary_remote_addr"` (per client IP).
Can be any NGINX variable, e.g. `"$http_x_forwarded_for"`.
key.title: key

nodelay.description: If `true`, burst requests are served immediately rather than delayed.
nodelay.title: nodelay

rate_limit.title: rate_limit

target_path.description:
Path to target on the upstream. If left empty, the default path
will be used.
target_path.title: target_path

target_port.description:
Port on the target upstream to target. Default port is the one
set on the parent `target_services` onject.
target_port.title: target_port

target_location.title: target_location

weight.description: NGINX upstream weight for weighted round-robin load distribution.
weight.title: weight

target_services.title: target_services

services.description: Map of service name → service config. Key becomes the service identifier.
services.title: services

items.description:
A single deployment definition as written in `config.yaml`.
One deployment maps to one cloud region/provider combination and owns
a set of named services.

deployment_name.description: Unique identifier for this deployment. Used in CLI `--target` flags and state files.
deployment_name.title: deployment_name

duplicate_deployment_name.description:
When set, this deployment is treated as a copy of the named deployment.
All services and settings are inherited and can be overridden.
duplicate_deployment_name.title: duplicate_deployment_name

env.description: Environment variables injected into every service in this deployment.
env.title: env

env_file.description: Path to a `.env` file whose contents are injected into every service.
env_file.title: env_file

location.description: Provider-specific datacenter/region slug, e.g. `"ash"` for Hetzner Ashburn.
location.title: location

pre_deployment.description: Commands run once on the relay server before any service setup begins.

file.description: Path to a shell script file on the target server to execute instead of `cmds`.
file.title: file

work_dir.description: Working directory for the commands or script.
work_dir.title: work_dir

pre_deployment.title: pre_deployment

relay_server_options.description: Options for the relay server provisioned for each deployment.

server_type.description: Override the default relay server hardware type (e.g. `"cx22"` on Hetzner).
server_type.title: server_type

relay_server_options.title: relay_server_options

additionalProperties.description:
Configuration for a single named service within a deployment.
A service represents one logical tier (e.g. `web`, `api`, `lb`).
The `type` field selects the deployment path; all other fields are
interpreted in the context of that type.

allow_ips.description:
Restrict inbound requests to these IPs/CIDRs only. All other IPs are
denied. Applies to all server blocks on this load balancer.

Accepts raw IPs/CIDRs or named providers which are automatically
expanded to their published ranges. Supported providers: "cloudflare".

Example: ["cloudflare", "5.78.1.245"]

Only applies to `load_balancer` services.
allow_ips.title: allow_ips

clusters.description:
Number of server clusters. During rolling updates, clusters are
deployed one at a time for zero-downtime. Defaults to `1`.
clusters.title: clusters

dependencies.description:
Packages to install on provisioned servers before running flight scripts.
Use `apt` for system packages, `turboci` for managed runtimes (bun, node, docker).
dependencies.title: dependencies

dir_mappings.description: Local → remote directory mappings synced before each deployment.

items.description: A local → remote directory sync mapping used during the prepare phase.

dst.description: Absolute destination path on target servers.
dst.title: dst

ignore_file.description: Path to an rsync-style ignore file.
ignore_file.title: ignore_file

ignore_patterns.description: Explicit patterns to exclude from the sync.
ignore_patterns.title: ignore_patterns

relay_ignore.description: Patterns excluded only on the local → relay leg of the sync.
relay_ignore.title: relay_ignore

src.description: Local source path (relative to the `.turboci` directory).
src.title: src

use_gitignore.description: If `true`, applies the project's `.gitignore` as rsync exclude rules.
use_gitignore.title: use_gitignore

dir_mappings.title: dir_mappings

duplicate_service_name.description:
When set, this service is treated as a copy of the named service
within the same deployment.
duplicate_service_name.title: duplicate_service_name

enable_public_ip.description:
When `true`, servers for this service receive a public IP in addition to
their private IP. Defaults to `false` — servers are private-only and
accessed exclusively through the relay.
enable_public_ip.title: enable_public_ip

env.description: Environment variables injected into this service's servers.
env.title: env

env_file.description: Path to a `.env` file loaded into this service's environment.
env_file.title: env_file

anyOf.description: Configuration for pulling a git repository onto target servers.

api_key.description: API key or personal access token for private repo access.
api_key.title: api_key

host.description: Custom git host, used for self-hosted Gitea/Gitlab instances.
host.title: host

keep_updated.description:
If `true`, TurboCI continuously polls the repository and re-runs
the flight scripts whenever new commits are detected.
keep_updated.title: keep_updated

paradigm.description: Git hosting provider. Affects clone URL construction and auth.
paradigm.title: paradigm

public_repo.description: If `true`, clones without authentication (no deploy key required).
public_repo.title: public_repo

work_dir.description:
Absolute path on target servers where the repo is cloned.
Defaults to `/app`.
work_dir.title: work_dir

items.description: Configuration for pulling a git repository onto target servers.

api_key.description: API key or personal access token for private repo access.
api_key.title: api_key

host.description: Custom git host, used for self-hosted Gitea/Gitlab instances.
host.title: host

keep_updated.description:
If `true`, TurboCI continuously polls the repository and re-runs
the flight scripts whenever new commits are detected.
keep_updated.title: keep_updated

paradigm.description: Git hosting provider. Affects clone URL construction and auth.
paradigm.title: paradigm

public_repo.description: If `true`, clones without authentication (no deploy key required).
public_repo.title: public_repo

work_dir.description:
Absolute path on target servers where the repo is cloned.
Defaults to `/app`.
work_dir.title: work_dir

git.description: Git repository source(s) to clone/pull onto target servers.
git.title: git

haproxy.description:
HAProxy proxy configuration (frontends, backends, health checks, stats).
Only used when `type` is `"haproxy"`.

balance.description:
Load balancing algorithm.
- `roundrobin` — each server in turn (default)
- `leastconn` — server with fewest active connections (best for databases)
- `first` — first available server (useful for active/passive failover)
- `source` — sticky sessions based on client IP
balance.title: balance

listen_port.description:
Port HAProxy's frontend listens on for client connections.
Defaults to `5432` for postgres mode, `3306` for mysql mode.
listen_port.title: listen_port

mode.description:
Proxy mode.
- `tcp` — layer-4 pass-through, required for databases (default)
- `http` — layer-7 HTTP proxy with header inspection
mode.title: mode

stats_password.description: Password for the HAProxy statistics page.
stats_password.title: stats_password

stats_port.description: Port for the HAProxy statistics web page. Defaults to `8404`.
stats_port.title: stats_port

stats_user.description: Username for the HAProxy statistics page.
stats_user.title: stats_user

target_services.description:
Backend services HAProxy will proxy traffic to.
Accepts any service type (postgres, mysql, mariadb-galera, etc.).

items.description:
A single backend server entry for an HAProxy service.
HAProxy is a generic TCP/HTTP proxy and can route to any service type.

backup.description: If `true`, this server is only used when all non-backup servers are down.
backup.title: backup

check.description: Health check settings for this backend server.

fall.description: Number of consecutive failures required to mark a server DOWN. Defaults to `3`.
fall.title: fall

interval.description:
Time between health checks (e.g. `"2s"`, `"500ms"`).
Defaults to `"2s"`.
interval.title: interval

rise.description: Number of consecutive successes required to mark a server UP. Defaults to `2`.
rise.title: rise

type.description:
Health check protocol.
- `tcp` — basic TCP connection check (works for any service)
- `http` — HTTP request check
- `mysql` — MySQL/MariaDB protocol handshake check
- `pgsql` — PostgreSQL startup message check
- `redis` — Redis PING check
type.title: type

check.title: check

port.description: Port the target service listens on.
port.title: port

service_name.description: Name of the service in the same deployment to proxy traffic to.
service_name.title: service_name

target_services.title: target_services

haproxy.title: haproxy

healthcheck.description: Healthcheck run after the service starts to verify it is live.
healthcheck.title: healthcheck

init.description: Shell commands run once on first deploy (not on re-deploys).
init.title: init

instances.description: Number of servers to provision per cluster. Defaults to `1`.
instances.title: instances

logs.description:
Commands or log sources tailed when `turboci control` streams logs
for this service.
logs.title: logs

mariadb_galera.description:
MariaDB Galera cluster configuration (cluster name, SST method, databases).
Only used when `type` is `"mariadb-galera"`.

bind_address.description:
Address to bind the MySQL listener to.
Use `"0.0.0.0"` to accept connections from all interfaces (required for
MaxScale or HAProxy to reach private cluster IPs). Defaults to `"0.0.0.0"`.
bind_address.title: bind_address

cluster_name.description:
`wsrep_cluster_name` — shared identifier all Galera nodes must agree on.
Defaults to `"turboci_galera_cluster"`.
cluster_name.title: cluster_name

databases.description: Databases and users to create after cluster bootstrap.

items.description:
A database and optional user to create during service initialisation.
Used by `mariadb-galera`, `postgres`, and `mysql` service types.

charset.description: Character set (e.g. `"utf8mb4"`). Only applies to MySQL/MariaDB.
charset.title: charset

user.description: Database user to create and grant privileges to this database.
user.title: user

databases.title: databases

port.description: MySQL-protocol port the cluster nodes listen on. Defaults to `3306`.
port.title: port

sst_method.description:
State Snapshot Transfer method used when a new node joins the cluster.
- `mariabackup` — hot backup via MariaDB Backup (recommended, no locks)
- `rsync` — rsync-based full copy (simple, blocks writes during transfer)
- `xtrabackup-v2` — Percona XtraBackup (for MySQL-based setups)
sst_method.title: sst_method

mariadb_galera.title: mariadb_galera

maxscale.description:
MaxScale proxy configuration (routers, monitors, admin API).
Only used when `type` is `"maxscale"`.

admin_port.description: Port for the MaxScale REST API and GUI. Defaults to `8989`.
admin_port.title: admin_port

admin_user.description: MaxScale admin REST API username. Defaults to `"admin"`.
admin_user.title: admin_user

listen_port.description:
MySQL-protocol port MaxScale listens on for client connections.
Defaults to `3306`.
listen_port.title: listen_port

monitor.description:
MaxScale monitor module.
- `galeramon` — for MariaDB Galera clusters (detects primary/donor/joiner state)
- `mariadbmon` — for MariaDB/MySQL primary-replica setups
- `mysqlmon` — legacy monitor for older MySQL setups
monitor.title: monitor

password.description: Password for the MaxScale database user.
password.title: password

router.description:
MaxScale router module.
- `readwritesplit` — routes writes to primary, distributes reads across replicas (default)
- `readconnroute` — simple round-robin connection routing
- `schemarouter` — routes by schema name (sharding)
router.title: router

target_services.description:
Backend services MaxScale will route connections to.
Typically points to a `mariadb-galera` or `mysql` service.

items.description:
A single backend server entry for a MaxScale proxy service.
Points to a `mariadb-galera` or `mysql` service in the same deployment.

backup.description: If `true`, this target is used only when all non-backup servers are unavailable.
backup.title: backup

port.description: MySQL-protocol port the target service listens on (typically `3306`).
port.title: port

service_name.description: Name of the service in the same deployment to route DB traffic to.
service_name.title: service_name

weight.description: Relative weight for connection distribution. Higher values receive more connections.
weight.title: weight

target_services.title: target_services

user.description:
Database user MaxScale uses for monitoring and service authentication.
This user must have `REPLICATION CLIENT`, `SHOW DATABASES`, and `SELECT` privileges.
user.title: user

maxscale.title: maxscale

mysql.description:
MySQL server configuration (port, users, replication).
Only used when `type` is `"mysql"`.

bind_address.description:
Address MySQL binds to.
Use `"0.0.0.0"` to accept connections from all interfaces (required for
ProxySQL or HAProxy to reach private server IPs). Defaults to `"0.0.0.0"`.
bind_address.title: bind_address

databases.description: Databases and users to create after initialisation.

items.description:
A database and optional user to create during service initialisation.
Used by `mariadb-galera`, `postgres`, and `mysql` service types.

charset.description: Character set (e.g. `"utf8mb4"`). Only applies to MySQL/MariaDB.
charset.title: charset

user.description: Database user to create and grant privileges to this database.
user.title: user

databases.title: databases

max_connections.description: Maximum number of simultaneous client connections. Defaults to `151`.
max_connections.title: max_connections

enabled.description: Enable MySQL binary-log replication. Defaults to `false`.
enabled.title: enabled

server_id.title: server_id

user.description: Replication user created on the primary. Defaults to `"replicator"`.
user.title: user

mysql.title: mysql

os.description: OS image slug to use when creating servers for this service.
os.title: os

postgres.description:
PostgreSQL server configuration (port, users, replication).
Only used when `type` is `"postgres"`.

databases.description: Databases and users to create after initialisation.

items.description:
A database and optional user to create during service initialisation.
Used by `mariadb-galera`, `postgres`, and `mysql` service types.

charset.description: Character set (e.g. `"utf8mb4"`). Only applies to MySQL/MariaDB.
charset.title: charset

user.description: Database user to create and grant privileges to this database.
user.title: user

databases.title: databases

listen_addresses.description:
Interfaces PostgreSQL listens on.
Use `"*"` to accept connections from all interfaces (required for HAProxy
to reach private server IPs). Defaults to `"localhost"`.
listen_addresses.title: listen_addresses

max_connections.description:
Maximum number of client connections. Defaults to `100`.
Increase when using a connection pooler like PgBouncer or HAProxy.
max_connections.title: max_connections

port.description: Port PostgreSQL listens on. Defaults to `5432`.
port.title: port

max_wal_senders.description:
Maximum number of concurrent WAL sender processes.
Should be ≥ the number of replica instances. Defaults to `5`.
max_wal_senders.title: max_wal_senders

user.description: Replication user created on the primary. Defaults to `"replicator"`.
user.title: user

shared_buffers.description:
Shared memory buffer size (e.g. `"256MB"`, `"1GB"`).
Rule of thumb: set to 25% of available RAM. Defaults to `"128MB"`.
shared_buffers.title: shared_buffers

postgres.title: postgres

proxysql.description:
ProxySQL proxy configuration (hostgroups, query rules, monitor user).
Only used when `type` is `"proxysql"`.

admin_port.description:
ProxySQL admin interface port (MySQL protocol, admin credentials required).
Defaults to `6032`.
admin_port.title: admin_port

listen_port.description:
MySQL-protocol port clients connect to on ProxySQL.
Defaults to `6033`.
listen_port.title: listen_port

monitor_password.description: Password for the ProxySQL monitor user.
monitor_password.title: monitor_password

monitor_user.description:
MySQL user ProxySQL uses to monitor backend health via `SELECT 1` queries.
This user needs `USAGE` privilege on the backend servers.
monitor_user.title: monitor_user

query_rules.description:
Query routing rules evaluated against incoming query digests.
Rules are applied in ascending `rule_id` order.

items.description:
A query routing rule for ProxySQL.
Rules are evaluated in ascending `rule_id` order; the first matching rule wins.

apply.description:
If `true`, stop evaluating further rules once this rule matches.
Defaults to `true`.
apply.title: apply

comment.description: Human-readable annotation stored in the ProxySQL rule table.
comment.title: comment

destination_hostgroup.description: Hostgroup to send matching queries to.
destination_hostgroup.title: destination_hostgroup

match_digest.description:
Regular expression matched against the normalised query digest.
Example: `"^SELECT"` matches all SELECT statements.
match_digest.title: match_digest

rule_id.description:
Numeric rule identifier. Lower numbers are evaluated first.
Defaults to insertion order if omitted.
rule_id.title: rule_id

query_rules.title: query_rules

reader_hostgroup.description:
Hostgroup ID for read traffic (SELECT).
Defaults to `20`.
reader_hostgroup.title: reader_hostgroup

target_services.description:
Backend services ProxySQL will route connections to.
Typically points to a `mysql` service. Use different `hostgroup` values
on target entries to separate writer and reader pools.

items.description:
A single backend server entry for a ProxySQL service.
Maps to one `mysql_servers` row in the ProxySQL admin database.

backup.description: If `true`, this server is placed in the OFFLINE_SOFT state initially.
backup.title: backup

hostgroup.description:
ProxySQL hostgroup ID. Use separate hostgroups for writers and readers
(e.g. writers: `10`, readers: `20`) to enable read/write splitting via query rules.
Defaults to `0`.
hostgroup.title: hostgroup

max_connections.description: Maximum number of connections ProxySQL will open to this backend server.
max_connections.title: max_connections

port.description: MySQL-protocol port the target service listens on (typically `3306`).
port.title: port

service_name.description: Name of the service in the same deployment to route traffic to.
service_name.title: service_name

weight.description: Relative connection weight within the hostgroup.
weight.title: weight

target_services.title: target_services

writer_hostgroup.description:
Hostgroup ID for write traffic (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DDL).
Defaults to `10`.
writer_hostgroup.title: writer_hostgroup

proxysql.title: proxysql

run.description: Preflight, start, and postflight shell scripts for this service.

postflight.description: Commands run after the service starts (smoke tests, cache warm-up, etc.).

file.description: Path to a shell script file on the target server to execute instead of `cmds`.
file.title: file

work_dir.description: Working directory for the commands or script.
work_dir.title: work_dir

postflight.title: postflight

preflight.description: Commands run before the service starts (install deps, migrations, etc.).

file.description: Path to a shell script file on the target server to execute instead of `cmds`.
file.title: file

work_dir.description: Working directory for the commands or script.
work_dir.title: work_dir

preflight.title: preflight

start.description: Commands that start the service process (must be non-blocking or daemonised).

file.description: Path to a shell script file on the target server to execute instead of `cmds`.
file.title: file

work_dir.description: Working directory for the commands or script.
work_dir.title: work_dir

start.title: start

work_dir.description: Default working directory for all scripts in this run block.
work_dir.title: work_dir

run.title: run

spec_url.description:
Yaml file url which defines the spec, used only when `type` is
set to `spec`. This can also be a path to a local file; absolute
and relative paths work the same.
spec_url.title: spec_url

ssl.description: SSL/TLS configuration. Triggers Certbot certificate provisioning.
ssl.title: ssl

target_services.description:
Target services this load balancer proxies to.
Only used when `type` is `"load_balancer"`.

items.description:
A single upstream target for a load balancer service.
Each target maps to one NGINX `upstream` block and one `server` block.

backup.description: If `true`, this target is used as a fallback when all primary servers are down.
backup.title: backup

domains.description:
Domains to attach to this target's server block. Triggers TLS termination
when `ssl` is configured on the parent service. Accepts plain strings or
`TCIConfigServiceDomain` objects.

anyOf.description: An explicitly named domain, used as an alternative to a plain domain string.

domains.title: domains

locations.description:
Custom NGINX `location` blocks rendered inside this target's server block.
The default `location /` proxy block is auto-generated unless a `"/"` path
is explicitly defined here.

items.description:
A custom NGINX `location` block rendered inside a load balancer server block.
By default each location proxies to the upstream; set `proxy: false` for
locations that should be handled locally (static files, redirects, etc.).

directives.description:
Arbitrary NGINX directives added verbatim inside this location block.
String values emit a single directive; array values emit one line per entry.

Example:
```yaml
directives:
  root: /var/www/html
  add_header:
    - "Cache-Control 'public'"
    - "X-Frame-Options DENY"
```
directives.title: directives

match.description:
NGINX location path or pattern.
Supports all NGINX matching prefixes: `"/"`, `"/api/"`, `"= /ping"`,
`"~* \\.(jpg|png)$"`, etc.
match.title: match

no_proxy.description:
If `true`, the upstream `proxy_pass` block is omitted for this location.
Use when serving static files, issuing redirects, or returning custom
responses. Defaults to `false`.
no_proxy.title: no_proxy

rate_limit.description: Rate limiting applied to this location.

burst.description: Burst queue size — requests exceeding the rate are queued up to this limit.
burst.title: burst

key.description:
NGINX rate limit key. Defaults to `"$binary_remote_addr"` (per client IP).
Can be any NGINX variable, e.g. `"$http_x_forwarded_for"`.
key.title: key

nodelay.description: If `true`, burst requests are served immediately rather than delayed.
nodelay.title: nodelay

rate_limit.title: rate_limit

target_path.description:
Path to target on the upstream. If left empty, the default path
will be used.
target_path.title: target_path

target_port.description:
Port on the target upstream to target. Default port is the one
set on the parent `target_services` onject.
target_port.title: target_port

locations.title: locations

port.description: Port the target service listens on.
port.title: port

service_name.description: Name of the service in the same deployment to proxy traffic to.
service_name.title: service_name

target_location.description: Custom NGINX `location` block added to the base("/") location

directives.description:
Arbitrary NGINX directives added verbatim inside this location block.
String values emit a single directive; array values emit one line per entry.

Example:
```yaml
directives:
  root: /var/www/html
  add_header:
    - "Cache-Control 'public'"
    - "X-Frame-Options DENY"
```
directives.title: directives

match.description:
NGINX location path or pattern.
Supports all NGINX matching prefixes: `"/"`, `"/api/"`, `"= /ping"`,
`"~* \\.(jpg|png)$"`, etc.
match.title: match

no_proxy.description:
If `true`, the upstream `proxy_pass` block is omitted for this location.
Use when serving static files, issuing redirects, or returning custom
responses. Defaults to `false`.
no_proxy.title: no_proxy

rate_limit.description: Rate limiting applied to this location.

burst.description: Burst queue size — requests exceeding the rate are queued up to this limit.
burst.title: burst

key.description:
NGINX rate limit key. Defaults to `"$binary_remote_addr"` (per client IP).
Can be any NGINX variable, e.g. `"$http_x_forwarded_for"`.
key.title: key

nodelay.description: If `true`, burst requests are served immediately rather than delayed.
nodelay.title: nodelay

rate_limit.title: rate_limit

target_path.description:
Path to target on the upstream. If left empty, the default path
will be used.
target_path.title: target_path

target_port.description:
Port on the target upstream to target. Default port is the one
set on the parent `target_services` onject.
target_port.title: target_port

target_location.title: target_location

weight.description: NGINX upstream weight for weighted round-robin load distribution.
weight.title: weight

target_services.title: target_services

services.description: Map of service name → service config. Key becomes the service identifier.
services.title: services

TCIConfigServiceConfigLog.description:
A log source entry for a service. Either a plain shell command string or
an object with an explicit `cmd` key.
TCIConfigServiceConfigLog.title: TCIConfigServiceConfigLog
